race.name: The Millars Gran Fondo
race.display_name: The Millars UCI Gran Fondo World Series
race.tagline: Spain's only UCI Gran Fondo World Series qualifier through Sierra de Espadán Natural Park

vitals.location: Castellón de la Plana, Castellón Province, Spain
vitals.entry_fee: Not specified; includes Gobik jersey, finisher medal, UCI medal for qualifiers
vitals.feed_zones: Drink stations, nutrition stations, mechanical assistance, medical support

climate.description: March average 18-20°C; ideal early-season conditions in Spain's second most mountainous province[1][2]

terrain.surface: 158.9 km loop starting/finishing at Avenida del Mar, Castellón de la Plana; follows Mijares River through 28 municipalities and Sierra de Espadán Natural Park; two major climbs: Coll de la Ibola (17 km, 3% avg to 580m), Coll de la Lloma (10 km, 6-8% initial to 593m)[1][2][4]
features: 9 km loop starting/finishing at Avenida del Mar, Castellón de la Plana
follows Mijares River through 28 municipalities and Sierra de Espadán Natural Park
two major climbs: Coll de la Ibola (17 km, 3% avg to 580m), Coll de la Lloma (10 km, 6-8% initial to 593m)[1][2][4]

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 79. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 77.

course_description.character: A single 157 km loop out of Castellón de la Plana, following the Mijares/Millars river corridor through more than 20 municipalities and into Serra d'Espadà Natural Park before returning to the coast. The route is front-loaded with climbing — the Coll de la Ibola grind comes early and the steeper Coll de la Lloma follows mid-race — then descends back to sea level for a flat run to the finish. There is no shorter medio fondo option; you ride the full 157 km or you don't start.
course_description.signature_challenge: Coll de la Lloma — roughly 10 km with an opening 5 km at 6-8%, the steepest sustained test on the course, arriving after Coll de la Ibola has already spent your legs.
suffering_zones: Coll de la Ibola — a long, steady 17 km grind at around 3% to 580 m that softens riders up early
Coll de la Lloma — the race's steepest climbing, 6-8% opening ramps to 593 m
Serra d'Espadà exposure — natural-park terrain with limited shelter from Mediterranean sun and wind

logistics.transport: Nearest airports: Valencia (VLC, 65km), Castellón-Costa Azahar (CDT). Start/finish Avenida del Mar, Castellón de la Plana. Full services: timing, expo, photos, video, meals, massages, mechanical support. UCI license required[1][2]

history.origin_story: Founded 2024 (inaugural March 10, Vila-Real); 1,400+ cyclists from 27 nations. 2024 winners: Lepoutre, de Vries; ambassadors Ivan Basso, Purito Rodriguez, Ibon Zugasti (category winner). Qualifiers advanced to Aalborg Worlds. 2025/2026 in Castellón de la Plana[1][2][4][5]

biased_opinion.summary: The Millars is barely three years old, but it already carries real weight: it's Spain's only UCI Gran Fondo World Series qualifier, and it sold out its 2026 edition at 2,000 riders from 36 nationalities (the 2024 debut drew 27 countries). The 157 km route through the Mijares river valley and Serra d'Espadà Natural Park is genuinely demanding — two real climbs, Coll de la Ibola and Coll de la Lloma, stacked into a single loop with no shorter option. This is a timed, UCI-licensed qualifier, not a casual cyclotourist ride, and the organization (ambassadors like Ivan Basso and Purito Rodríguez, a growing international field) is building fast. What it lacks is longevity — a three-year track record and a still-provisional route that can shift with roadworks.
strengths: Genuine UCI Gran Fondo World Series qualifier — a fast top-25% in your age group sends you to Worlds
Only one route: 157 km with two real climbs, so there's no soft option diluting the field
International draw from 27 countries in its 2024 debut, sold out at 2,000 riders from 36 nationalities by year three
Ambassador roster (Ivan Basso, Purito Rodríguez, Fabio Aru) signals real investment from the organizer
weaknesses: Young event — only three editions as of 2026, so there's limited track record on consistency year to year
No shorter medio fondo option — riders who can't handle 157 km and two climbs have nowhere to go but DNF
Non-federation-licensed riders pay a €12 surcharge on top of registration, plus optional €20 cancellation insurance
The official route is explicitly described as provisional and can change for roadworks or other contingencies
biased_opinion.bottom_line: If you want a serious, timed UCI qualifier on a genuinely hard Spanish coastal-mountain course and don't mind a young organizer still building its reputation, The Millars delivers. It is not a beginner's gran fondo — there's no shorter route to fall back on.

final_verdict.one_liner: Spain's only UCI Gran Fondo World Series qualifier through Sierra de Espadán Natural Park
final_verdict.should_you_race: Yes if you want a UCI-qualifying result and can handle 157 km with two real climbs and no bailout distance. Skip if you're newer to gran fondos and want a shorter option, or if you want a race with decades of established reputation behind it.
final_verdict.alternatives: If you want a longer-established Spanish UCI qualifier with a shorter distance option: Aitana Tour (Alicante). If you want the Mediterranean-coast climbing test without the UCI qualifier pressure: look at regional Comunitat Valenciana cyclotourist rides.

videos.title: 🔥 Millars UCI Gran Fondo de Castellón  con IBON ZUGASTI🚴💨
videos.description:
🚲 ¡Bienvenidos, DISFRUTONES DEL CICLISMO!

En este video os traigo una experiencia brutal en la Millars Gran Fondo de Castellón, una marcha cicloturista UCI con 158 km y 2.200 metros de desnivel. Pero lo mejor de todo… ¡compartiendo kilómetros con el bicharraco Ibon Zugasti! 🔥

Nos enfrentamos a los puertos de Aín y el Salto del Caballo, con rampas durísimas, paisajes espectaculares y un ritmo muy exigente ¿Cómo fue rodar junto a una leyenda del ciclismo? ¡Os lo cuento todo en el video!

⚠️ Bajadas con máxima precaución para disfrutar de la jornada con seguridad.

👦 Sobre mí: soy padre de tres hijas y ciclista desde hace 7 años. Me apasiona este deporte y trato de ayudar a quienes empiezan, porque sé lo duro que pueden ser los comienzos.

📲 Sígueme en mis redes sociales:
📸 Instagram: @fnz1986
🐦 Twitter: @FranMorcillo
🎵 TikTok: @fran.morcillo
🚴 Strava: Fran Morcillo

Si te gusta el contenido, ¡suscríbete y dale a like! 🚴‍♂️💨
videos.curation_reason: Recent first-person race experience video featuring the challenging 158km course with 2,200m elevation and key climbs including Aín and Salto del Caballo.

videos.title: Castellón Bike Events
videos.description:
Reunimos a organizadores de las siete pruebas de ciclismo en la provincia de Castellón que congregan a visitantes de todo el mundo en Emebikes.

Participaron: Publicom Sport Events, Gigante de Piedra, Desafio Maestrat, Mediterránea TRI, Castellón Gravel Race, Trixilxes, Millars UCI Gran Fondo y Sebastián Mora, ciclista profesional y actual campeón del mundo en pista.
